questions.question: Explain the differences between null pointers, void pointers, and wild pointers in C++. Write a C++ program demonstrating how to safely convert a void pointer to an int pointer and access the value. Also explain why checking for null is essential before dereferencing pointers.
keyPoints: Null pointer points to memory address 0, indicates no valid address
Void pointer is a generic pointer that can hold address of any data type
Wild pointer points to uninitialized or random memory
Type casting required for void pointer dereferencing
Null checks prevent segmentation faults

questions.question: Explain the three types of const pointers in C++: (a) Pointer to constant, (b) Constant pointer, and (c) Constant pointer to constant. Write code examples demonstrating each type with appropriate use cases.
keyPoints: Pointer to const: int const* p or const int* p - data is constant, pointer can change
Constant pointer: int* const p - pointer is constant, data can change
Const pointer to const: const int* const p - both pointer and data are constant
Use const for data protection and function parameter passing
Const correctness improves code readability and prevents bugs

questions.question: Compare and contrast raw pointers with smart pointers (unique_ptr, shared_ptr, weak_ptr) in modern C++. Write a program demonstrating memory leak prevention using unique_ptr for dynamic memory allocation. Explain the concept of RAII and how smart pointers implement it.
keyPoints: Raw pointers require manual memory management and cleanup
unique_ptr: exclusive ownership, automatically deallocates when out of scope
shared_ptr: shared ownership using reference counting
weak_ptr: non-owning reference to shared_ptr, breaks circular references
RAII (Resource Acquisition Is Initialization) ensures automatic resource cleanup
Smart pointers prevent memory leaks and dangling pointers

questions.question: Explain the relationship between pointers and arrays in C++. Demonstrate pointer arithmetic with an integer array. Write a function that uses pointer arithmetic to calculate the sum of array elements. Also explain why pointer arithmetic behaves differently for different data types.
keyPoints: Array name decays to pointer to first element
Pointer arithmetic: adding 1 moves by sizeof(data_type) bytes
arr[i] is equivalent to *(arr + i)
Pointers can be used to traverse arrays efficiently
Different data types have different pointer arithmetic increments
Pointer arithmetic is undefined when pointer goes beyond array bounds
